calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-08-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brianna Mannion, via ZOOM, for San Francisco Public Administrator, Administrator; Thomas Wrobel, via ZOOM, for Wilma Bradshaw, Petitioner; Konstatin Demiris, via ZOOM, for Inheritance Funding, Inc.; Matt Millim, via ZOOM on behalf of Inheritance Funding, Inc.; Rick Lloyd, via ZOOM, Beneficiary; Robert Mason Lord, via ZOOM, Beneficiary.

The court begins by confirming with Mr. Wrobel that his client does not object to this petition. The court then confirms that an objection from Inheritance Funding, Inc. was filed. Mr. Demiris reports that his client was contacted by Kenneth Jones requesting an advance of distribution, a few weeks prior to today's hearing date. Mr. Demiris reports that his client advised Mr. Jones several times that as a result of this last-minute assignment, Mr. Jones would receive much less than he would be entitled to, if he waited for distribution. Regardless of the preceding, Mr. Jones requested the funds. Mr. Demiris then advises that his objection seeks to object to the petition only with regard to Mr. Jones' distributive share and the share due to his client. Mr. Demiris then confirms that Mr. Jones will be receiving a rebate from his client, as soon as distribution is received. The court advises Mr. Demiris that it will likely calculate his clients share and that of Mr. Jones so that the totals will be reflected in the order and thus a rebate to Mr. Jones will not be required. Mr. Wrobel then reports regarding Mr. Jones' urgency to request and accept the funds from Probate Advance. The court then queries Ms. Mannion regarding the discrepancy of the reserve being requested. Ms. Mannion confirms that the request is for a reserve of $9500. The court then waives the defect in the petition prayer which requests a reserve of $6500. The court then queries Ms. Mannion regarding the way that that her office calculates fee for bonds. Ms. Mannion offers oral testimony regarding the calculation of the bond fee which will be prorated to today's date. Ms. Mannion then offers to file a supplemental declaration to summarize the preceding. The court confirms that the request for bond will be less than what is proposed in the petition and asks if there are any objections. None are presented. Ms. Mannion advises that she is willing to submit a revised proposed order to the court. The court accepts Ms. Mannion's offer and asks that it be addressed to the assigned Examiner.

The accounting petition is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Estate Following Court?S Determination Regarding Gaither Lloyd, Aka Lord
Off Calendar Jun-29-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel, via ZOOM, counsel for Wilma Bradshaw, Petitioner.

The court begins by querying counsel regarding waivers of notice from those as listed in the Examiner's notes. Counsel confirms the filing of waivers of notice of hearing for all heirs in question. Counsel also reports that he has spoken to counsel for the San Francisco Public Administrator's office and confirmed that they do not oppose this petition.

After additional discussion, the Petition to Determine Persons Entitled to Distribution is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
Off Calendar Dec-15-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brianna Mannion appeared by zoom for San Francisco Public Administrator.
Thomas Wrobel appeared by zoom for Wilma Bradshaw (Claimant).
Debbie Jones appeared by telephone for Kathryn Cain (Objector).

Ms. Mannion stated that she has nothing to add, that she filed a declaration on Friday and that she wanted to make sure the Court received it. Mr. Wrobel and Ms. Jones presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not Gaither is the Son of Alpheus and therefore, the Decedent's (Alfred) half-brother. Discussion was also held regarding the issue of whether California Law, or North Carolina Law should govern. The Court stated that it is clear that North Carolina Law should govern. The Petition to Determine Persons Entitled to Distribution of Estate is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-09-2020 Continued To Nov-17-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-05-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brianna Mannion for San Francisco Public Administrator/Special Administrator.
Thomas Wrobel for Wilma Bradshaw (Petitioner).
William Walker appeared by telephone for Kathryn Cain (Petitioner).

The Court noted that there is an agreement by all Parties to grant the petition of the Public Administrator. All Counsel responded "correct". Mr. Walker and Mr. Wrobel stated that they have no objection to Ms. Cain's and Ms. Bradshaw's petitions going off calendar. The Court noted that all of the filed documents/petitions will remain in the Court's file. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration filed by the Public Administrator (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required. Status on close of estate is set for August 4, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration needs to be filed by July 28, 2021. The Petitions for Letters of Administration (2) filed by Kathryn Cain and Wilma Bradshaw are off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-05-2020 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, LIMITED APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-05-2020 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-11-2019 Continued To Feb-05-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brianna Mannion for San Francisco Public Administrator (Special Administrator).
Gary Gorski for Kathryn Cain (Petitioner).
Thomas Wrobel for Wilma Bradshaw (Petitioner).

The Court noted that there are competing petitions, stated its position regarding priority, that it does not think either side has a clear play and ordered the matter to mediation. The Court also informed Counsel that if the matter does not settle, its tentative is to appoint the Public Administrator. Ms. Mannion stated that mediation was raised at the last hearing, that there was an issue as to whether the 164 heirs are bound to mediation and that she was told by Mr. Walker that if Mr. Wrobel withdraws his petition, he is okay with going forward with the Public Administrator. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than January 24, 2020. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by January 28, 2020. The Petitions for Letters of Administration (3) are continued to and the Parties are to return on February 5, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. Ms. Mannion stated that the one-year statute runs in March, that they have not given notice to creditors and that she will submit an order extending special administration to February 5th.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.

Off Calendar Oct-30-2019 Continued To Dec-11-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-30-2019 Continued To Dec-11-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Gary Gorski specially appearing for William Walker, esq. for Kathryn Cain (Objector).
Tom Wrobel for Wilma Ira Bradshaw (Petitioner).
Brianna Mannion for San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA).

Ms. Mannion stated there are evidentiary determination to be made, requested Special Administrator, SFPA, to have special letters extended with additional powers to marshal assets and collect accounts in a timely manner and added that Wells Fargo Bank allowed $65,000.00 to be withdrawn. The Court and Counsel discuss the possibility of mediation. Mr. Wrobel stated the best available evidence is already before the court, i.e. 1920 census record demonstrating the relationship to decedent, agrees to 30 days continuance and to extending Special Administrator. Mr. Gorski stated Ms. Cain would agree to mediation. Counsel discuss choice of law and getting North Carolina law to apply. Ms. Mannion presents proposed order extending special administrator to opposing counsel, both Mr. Gorski and Mr. Wrobel agree with the expanded powers. Order extending special administrator with additional powers to expire on Dec. 11, 2019 is granted and signed in open court.

The competing Petitions for Letters of Administration (3) are continued to December 11, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for parties to submit briefing on choice of law question with request to take judicial notice of laws in question by November 27, 2019.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Appearances: Brianna Mannion for San Francisco Public Administrator.

A hearing was held for the purpose of extending the letters of special administration. The Court signed an order extending the special letters to October 30, 2019. The Petition for Letters of Administration is also continued to that date.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-18-2019 Continued To Oct-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, LIMITED APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-18-2019 Continued To Oct-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: William Walker appeared by telephone for Kathryn Cain (Petitioner).
Thomas Wrobel for Wilma Bradshaw (Petitioner).
Brianna Mannion for San Francisco Public Administrator.

The Court noted that there are three competing petitions. Mr. Wrobel stated that they are hoping to find additional evidence, that there is a record of a 1961 divorce that may have some records that are relevant to the situation, and that legitimacy is sufficient for the Court to make a determination that his Client is the closest Heir to the Decedent and should be appointed. The Court informed the Parties that documents do not exist unless the Court receives courtesy copies and reminds Counsel that they are required to provide copies of all filed documents in advance of the hearing. The Court also stated that the documents seem to support that Alpheus and Elva are the Parents of Gaither and that has seen no evidence of Gaither and Alfred being stepbrothers. Mr. Walker stated that the divorce paper work has no reference to any Children or evidence that Mr. Lord is the biological Father. Ms. Mannion noted that the Agency (Public Administrator) is still on the hook for this, requested a short continuance, stated that the apartment has been sealed for eight months and that the Landlord is being cooperative, but this is delaying clear out of the apartment. The Petitions for Letters of Administration (3) are continued to October 16, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Wrobel suggested that the Public Administrator be appointed as a Special Administrator. Ms. Mannion stated that she has no objection to her Client being appointed as the Special Administrator for the limited purpose of clearing out the apartment, but noted that it is only a one month continuance. Mr. Walker stated that he is okay with the Public Administrator being the Special Administrator if the Court feels it is necessary. Ms. Mannion raised the issue of not collecting assets and there being are no funds to pay for the clear out or passed rents. The Court appoints the Public Administrator as Special Administrator of the estate on its own motion. Ms. Mannion will provide a form of order.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-21-2019 Continued To Sep-18-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, LIMITED APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-21-2019 Continued To Sep-18-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Brianna Mannion for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA). Thomas Wrobel for Wilma Bradshaw. Benjamin Eagleton appearing by CourtCall for Kathryn Cain.

Mr. Eagleton stated that his client was nominated by a relative (Nancy Icard), that they have not filed a petition yet, that his appearance today is to object, that their petition will be filed right away, and that their objection is centered on the priority of appointment. The Court stated that it would be nice to have a written objection. Mr. Eagleton stated that he could file points and authorities. Ms. Mannion stated that this matter was referred to them by the medical examiner because there were no known relatives, that the apartment is currently locked up/sealed, that so far there are no issues with the landlord, but that they need to deal with this residence issue. Mr. Eagleton confirmed for Mr. Wrobel that he is a California attorney. The Court stated that it would allow a brief continuance for the objections. Ms. Mannion stated that the SFPA takes no position, that there is a priority to act over creditors, that there are so many heirs in this case, that a relative would have priority to act, and that they would not be willing to withdraw because of any possibility of having to refile. The Court noted that it wanted all three petitions heard had the same time. The Court and counsel confer regarding the new date. The Court stated that the continuance would allow time for Mr. Eagleton to file his objections, file his petition, and publish. The Court then continued the Petition for Letters of Administration (filed 6/17/19 by the Public Administrator) and the Petition for Letters of Administration (filed on 7/8/19 by Wilma Bradshaw) to September 18, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Hilton stated that Mr. Eagleton should inform the filing clerk that the date of September 18, 2019 has been authorized as the date for his petition.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Probate Examiner: Probate Director Sandy Hilton. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Matter not reported.
